item.title: 【used】Warwick / Streamer Stage I 4st Natural 1997 3.875kg #J-040997-97【Consignment】【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $4,051
item.description:
$4,051 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Warwick is a globally renowned guitar and bass brand based in Germany.

In recent years, they have begun distributing other world-class brands such as Sadowsky.

They have increased their presence in many fields and release numerous attractive products.

Many top artists, including Stuart Zender, Jonas Hellborg, and Robert Trujillo, use Warwick basses, showcasing their excellence to the world. (Titles omitted)

While the company offers several models, this is the Streamer Stage I, their most popular style.

The ergonomically designed body provides excellent playability for the player.

The body and neck are believed to be made of maple, and the fretboard of wenge.

The pickups are Active MEC P/J pickups.

The preamp features Active MEC electronics with a 3-band EQ.

The sound is tight and fast, typical of Warwick.

The clear attack, primarily due to the maple construction, combined with the tight response characteristic of a wenge fingerboard, produces a well-defined sound.

The P/J pickup layout combined with a 3-band EQ allows for a wide range of tones, from thick and punchy to sharp and clear.

This guitar combines the meticulous craftsmanship typical of 1990s German-made Warwicks with highly practical sound-shaping capabilities.

Fret life is approximately 70%.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork show some minor dents and scratches on the back. There is a noticeable dent of about 10.0mm near the control on the top of the body. There is also a dent of about 5.0mm near the strap pin.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume/Balancer, Treble, Middle, Bass
Nut Width: 38.8mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 1997
Country of Manufacture: Germany

This instrument will be delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by a GIB craftsman.

This is a consignment item, so shipping costs may differ from the usual.

Accessories: Soft Case (ARIA PB-EB)

Text: Mizune Ishikawa

Inspection 담당: Hirayama

Product Photos: Tei

GIB 3-Month Warranty Included

◯GIB, TONIQ Warranty Information

●Lifetime Free Maintenance

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free "String Vibration Setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

・Please present your warranty card with your name filled in.

・This warranty is only valid for the person who purchased the instrument at a GIB or TONIQ physical store or online shop. *Please note that you will be responsible for shipping and other related expenses.

*Repurchase Price Guarantee

If you sell your purchased item within 6 months of purchase, we guarantee the purchase price. The price is 50% for new items and 70% for used items.

*Defect Warranty

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. Excluding some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.

The warranty period is 3 months for used items and 1 year for new items.

Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:

* Malfunctions and damage caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.

* Replacement of consumables.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual.

* Malfunctions or damage to parts repaired or modified by parties other than our company.

* If the warranty card does not have your name, model name, serial number, etc. filled in, or if the text has been altered.

* If the warranty card is not presented or is lost.

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan.)

● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in

When you purchase an instrument, you will receive a 10% discount on the sales price when you use our trade-in service. (Consignment items are excluded. This discount applies only if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the sales price.)

Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.

We ship with sufficient packaging.

We also ship internationally.

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: Yamaha TRBX605FM Natural Satin — $653.99
item.description:
$653.99 · Very Good · bassNguitar

The Yamaha TRBX605FM is a 5-string bass offering outstanding value for money. It features an alder body with a flamed maple veneer, a 5-piece bolt-on maple/mahogany neck with 24 frets, and a rosewood fingerboard. Equipped with dual Alnico V pickups and an active/passive 3-band EQ preamp, it delivers crisp highs, tight lows, and punchy mids, making it a versatile instrument for a wide range of musical styles. Presented in very good condition.


More pix and details upon request.

Shipping worldwide available.

item.title: Fender 75th Anniversary Commemorative Precision Bass 2021 - 2-Color Bourbon Burst — $2,177.02
item.description:
$2,177.02 · Excellent · BassBros

In 1946 Clarence Leonidas "Leo" Fender created Fender Electric Instrument Company and changed the trajectory of modern music forever. The 75th Anniversary Series celebrates Leo’s bold, revolutionary designs with special versions of his most famous creations.

The 75th Anniversary Commemorative Precision Bass is decked out in a beautiful 2-Color Bourbon Burst finish over an ash body with gold hardware. Ash is a very resonant wood and really adds to to tone of this Bass. The one-piece maple neck features a '63 Precision Bass neck profile, rolled fingerboard edges, a "Super-Natural" satin finish and a sculpted neck heel for unsurpassed comfort and playability.

Custom Shop ’62 P Bass pickups offer vintage-style growl, mid-range and clarity while the HiMass Vintage bridge enhances sustain, resonance and stability. Finally, every Commemorative Precision Bass features special badging with a 75th anniversary ingot inlaid into the back of the headstock and an engraved anniversary neck plate.

This bass is in excellent condition with a few small signs of use. We have given the bass a full health check including checking trussrod, electronics, etc. Everything works perfectly as it should. Included is a hardcase.

– Part Exchange Welcome –

Serial Number – US20095096
Year – 2021
Colour – Bourbon Burst
Body Finish – Gloss
Body Wood – Ash
Neck Finish – Satin
Neck Wood – Maple
Fretboard – Maple
Frets – 20
Scale Length – 34″
Weight – 9lbs2oz/4.2kgs
Electronics – Passive
Pickups – Fender

item.title: Fender Custom Shop Pino Palladino Signature Precision Bass 2024 - Fiesta red — $5,956.18
item.description:
$5,956.18 · Excellent · Ofertas en equipos de Adrián

I am offering for sale this extraordinary Fender Custom Shop Pino Palladino Signature Precision Bass, manufactured in 2024, in absolutely pristine condition.

I am the original owner, and the bass is practically brand new. It has barely been played, has always been kept at home, carefully maintained, and shows no signs of wear. It comes with its original Fender Custom Shop case, Certificate of Authenticity (COA), and all original documentation and accessories. To speak of this instrument is to speak of one of the most admired and highly rated Precision Basses ever made by the Fender Custom Shop. This is no ordinary Precision; it is a recreation of the legendary bass belonging to Pino Palladino—one of the most influential session musicians of recent decades—built to his personal specifications by the Fender Custom Shop. It features a lightweight select alder body, a maple neck with a profile inspired by his vintage instruments, a rosewood fingerboard, a nitrocellulose finish, a Custom Shop pickup wound specifically for this model, and stock Thomastik-Infeld flatwound strings. Its sound is simply exceptional: deep, warm, round, and nuanced, with rich, musical mids that allow every note to sit perfectly in the mix. It is the classic Precision Bass sound taken to its ultimate expression: elegant, organic, and possessing extraordinary dynamics. It is an instrument particularly prized by players of: - Soul - R&B - Blues - Fingerstyle funk - Pop - Classic rock - Studio and session work In these genres, it delivers a tone that is hard to match, with a musicality that has made this model a benchmark Precision Bass among professional bassists. It enjoys an excellent reputation. On specialized forums and among owners, it is frequently described as one of the best modern Precision Basses from the Fender Custom Shop, with particular praise for its feel, tonal balance, and build quality. It is a highly sought-after model, produced in limited quantities and in constant demand among musicians and collectors alike. This is a magnificent opportunity to acquire a practically new instrument—without the wait—in exceptional condition. If you are looking for one of the best Precision Basses Fender Custom Shop has produced in recent years, you would be hard-pressed to find a better opportunity.

Product Details

Fender American Professional II Jazz Bass

The Fender American Professional II Jazz Bass, an updated version of the traditional Jazz Bass built on the foundation of its ease of use and adapted for modern playing environments, has arrived.

It features an alder body, maple neck, and maple fingerboard, resulting in a classic 34-inch scale length.

Equipped with V-Mod II Single-Coil Jazz Bass pickups, the controls are simple: separate Volume and Master Tone controls for the front and rear pickups.

With a 9.5-inch radius, 38.1mm nut width, 19mm string spacing, and 20 frets, it achieves both the slim, comfortable grip characteristic of traditional Jazz Basses and ease of tone shaping.

When plugged into an amplifier, it delivers the tight bass and clear mid-to-high frequencies characteristic of a Jazz Bass. Fingerstyle playing provides clear tonal definition, while pick playing delivers a forward attack.

This guitar offers a well-balanced sound when blending the two single-coil pickups, making it easy to express nuances through picking dynamics and right-hand position.

It's a perfect choice for those who want both classic Fender sound and modern playability.

It's also ideal for quickly shaping your sound during live performances or recordings, making it a reliable option for players who want to practically use a standard JB bass.

Please consider this opportunity.

[Specifications]
Body: Alder
Neck: Maple
Fingerboard: Maple
Fingerboard Radius: 9.5 inch-R

Product Details

The Squier by Fender Affinity Jazz Bass is a Jazz Bass-style model belonging to the Affinity Series of Squier, a brand directly affiliated with Fender. It combines an accessible price range with a classic look, making it easy to incorporate into both practice and band performances.

This model features a poplar body, maple neck, bolt-on construction, 34-inch scale length, and 20 frets. The nut width is approximately 38.1mm, and the slim neck profile makes it easy to grip, even for beginners. The traditional layout of two ceramic single-coil Jazz Bass pickups, two volume controls, and one tone control allows for intuitive sound shaping.

The sound is characterized by a well-defined bass tone typical of a Jazz Bass, and a clarity that won't get lost in an ensemble. The neck pickup provides a thick, mellow bassline, while the bridge pickup allows for easy adjustment to create a tighter, more attack-driven tone. This bass is versatile enough to handle a wide range of playing styles, from fingerstyle to pick playing and slap bass, making it easy to use in many genres including pop, rock, and funk.

It's recommended for those who want to easily start playing the classic jazz bass style, or those looking for a versatile secondary bass with minimal quirks. Despite its simple configuration, it offers a wide range of tonal possibilities, making it a natural choice for daily practice, live performances, and recording.

When fingerstyle, it's easy to produce a full tone while maintaining definition, and when picked, the attack is easily projected.

With two SJB-1 pickups, it offers a well-balanced sound that ranges from the thickness of the neck pickup to the clarity of the bridge pickup.

Recommended for those seeking the ease of use of a JB-style bass, along with the stability and practical sound characteristic of Japanese-made instruments.

It features open-type Spector PJ-style pickups combined with }12dB active tone control.

As expected from a PJ configuration, this bass allows for easy toning, whether you add tightness at the bridge or thickness at the neck, adapting to different situations.

Combining a striking appearance with practical sound shaping, this model is a versatile instrument suitable for a wide range of genres, particularly rock and pop.

The YAMAHA TRBX174 is a 4-string passive bass guitar in Yamaha's TRBX series. Despite its simple configuration, it boasts an easy-to-handle body balance and long scale, making it accessible to everyone from beginners to those looking for a secondary bass for home recording.

It features a mahogany body, maple neck, and rosewood fingerboard, with a nut width of 40mm and a scale length of 863.6mm. The 24-fret design allows for a wide range of tones. The passive configuration with two volume and one tone controls makes it easy to intuitively shape your sound without overcomplicating it.

When plugged into an amplifier, it offers a straightforward passive response and a quick attack that allows for easy expression of picking dynamics. The bass is well-defined, and the tone is easy to grasp, making it easy to play with both fingerstyle and pick techniques. This guitar is easy to follow within a band setting and can be used for a wide range of purposes, from practice to live performance.

This model is perfect for those who prioritize practicality and ease of playability from the moment they pick it up, rather than complex features. It boasts Yamaha's characteristic solid construction and the ease of use unique to passive 4-string guitars, making it a very user-friendly instrument for everyday use.

Product Details

TEISCO Spectrum Bass: A Red Bizarre Bass / Short-Scale Design

The TEISCO Spectrum Bass is a striking 4-string bass with an original body shape and a red color. This particular model is a solid-body Spectrum Bass, perfect for those who want a look different from typical basses.

This model boasts the easy handling characteristic of TEISCO basses, thanks to its short scale. It's a passive model with a simple configuration featuring two pickups, volume, tone, and individual pickup on/off switches. Based on similar information, the fingerboard is rosewood and it has 20 frets.

When plugged into an amp, it has a simple, solid attack and a distinctive presence in the midrange. Rather than a modern, well-defined wide range, it easily showcases the rawness of picking and the contours of phrases, making it well-suited for garage rock and lo-fi sound shaping.

With scratches and replaced parts, this bass has a strong individual character, offering a unique look and a simple passive sound that truly embodies the bizarre bass aesthetic. Recommended for those looking for a unique bass guitar with a practical design.

Please consider this opportunity.

Product Details

FERNANDES FRB-55

The FERNANDES FRB-55 is an easy-to-handle bass guitar with a slightly more compact 32-inch scale.

It fits comfortably in the hand and doesn't strain the hand movements, making it easy to play even for extended periods.

This model features a basic configuration of a basswood body, maple neck, and rosewood fingerboard, and is finished with 21 frets.

The pickup is in a PJ configuration, and it uses 1 volume/balance/treble/bass control. This makes it easy for the player to adjust the sound blend and fine-tune the tone to suit different genres and phrases.

The sound has a smooth attack and responds clearly to picking and fingering, naturally bringing out the contours of phrases.

With its ease of handling and user-friendly sound character, it's an excellent choice for a first bass, or for anyone looking for a practical instrument that's easy to use.

Please consider this opportunity.

Wefve just received a rare Fender bass model on the used market!

The gAmerican Deluxeh series, which debuted in the late f90s and incorporated cutting-edge modern specifications, is the predecessor to todayfs Ultra series.
The model wefve just received is the gDimension Bass,h released in 2013 as part of that series.

Introduced as a model following in the footsteps of the classic Jazz Bass and Precision Bass, the Dimension Bass features a rounded offset body, a single humbucker, and a 3-band EQ?a distinctive configuration within the Fender lineup.
The fingerboard features a compound radius ranging from 9.5" to 14", and combined with the active circuitry, it incorporates modern specifications.

While it delivers the powerful sound typical of a humbucker, it doesnft come across as overly assertive, making it a highly versatile instrument.

This 1998 Music Man Stingray SR-4 is considered the epitome of active-bass guitars.

It features an ash body, a maple neck with a 6-screw joint, a maple fingerboard, an 864mm (34") scale length, a nut width of approximately 43.1mm, a humbucker pickup, and a 3-band equalizer.

item.description:
$1,077.07 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

This is a Fender Japan Exclusive 60s Jazz Bass with US-style pickups, manufactured in 2015.

A Jazz Bass that recreates the classic 60s style. It features specifications based on the Fender Japan JB62US, with an alder body, maple neck, slab-jointed rosewood fingerboard, two J-BASS VINTAGE (USA) pickups, and a spiral saddle bridge.

This model is characterized by its rich, full-bodied sound, achieved through the combination of an alder body and USA pickups, and is highly recommended for home recording.

There are signs of use (cosmetic damage such as dents near the bridge, minor scratches and scuffs, wear and wipe marks, picking marks, ring marks on the back of the neck, and tarnish or pitting on metal parts and screws).

Set up upon arrival (cleaning, string replacement, truss rod adjustment, octave adjustment, string height adjustment, and fret leveling); the frets are at a height suitable for playing, and the truss rod has been tested to 60 degrees in both directions and is in good working order.

item.title: Fender Japan JB62-60 VWH (Vintage White) Fender Japan [E-44040] (06/29) — $941.63
item.description:
$941.63 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

This is the Fender Japan JB62-60 (manufactured by Fujigen), a 60s-style jazz bass that follows the classic '62 design.

It features a basswood body, a maple neck, a slab-jointed rosewood fingerboard, and is equipped with a JB Single (JAPAN) pickup (*according to catalog specifications), with a 34-inch long scale and 20 frets.

With a look that has developed a distinguished patina over time, this instrument offers the classic Jazz Bass sound that truly shines in an ensemble. Itfs highly recommended for beginners and intermediate players alike, and is also a valuable asset for home recording.

The strap pin on the back of the headstock has been removed, and the serial number at the neck joint has been sanded off. There are signs of wear (significant discoloration, dents and paint chips around the perimeter, small scratches and scuff marks, scuffs and wipe marks, picking marks, ring marks on the back of the neck, paint cracks at the joint, tarnish, pitting, and rust on metal parts and screws, and other cosmetic damage).

It was set up upon arrival (cleaning, string replacement, truss rod adjustment, octave adjustment, string height adjustment, fret leveling, sticker removal, and ground wire installation), the fret tops are at a height suitable for playing, and the truss rod has been tested up to 60 degrees in both directions and is in good working order.

item.title: Ibanez BTB7-NT 2013 (limited edition - rare) — $1,447.67
item.description:
$1,447.67 · Very Good · Ollie

I‘ve had this bass from new when it came out in 2013. This is one the Ibanez limited spot release models right before they started doing the bass workshop models in 2014 - which had a slightly different version of this bass with a natural flat finish and the grey metal hardware.

I‘ve used this bass extensively over the past 13 years, live and in the studio, so there‘s a bit of buckle rash on the back, the gold colour has worn off some of the hardware, there’s a small dent on top of the body and there‘s some wear on the pickups (all pictured).

Overall, this bass is fantastic. The cap-10 active pickups sound great and I’ve found it particularly good at doubling guitar parts. The scale length is 35 inches, it has a ’monorail’ bridge, and it says in tune for ages. I’m only selling because I haven’t used it in a few years and I’m hoping it‘ll go to a good home.

item.title: BanG Dream! BOTTOM BUMP PJ NANAMI MODEL See-Through Black [GC2407664] (06/29) — $541.76
item.description:
$541.76 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A value-for-money version of the Morfonica bassist Nanami Hiromachi model.
This original model is based on the ESP BOTTOM BUMP and features a Recostone Morpho butterfly inlay on the fingerboard.

It features an alder body with a 3-piece hard maple neck and a rosewood fingerboard, configured with 21 frets.

The control layout is the same as the ESP version, but it does not include an active 3-band EQ; instead, it features volume and tone controls for each pickup, along with an on/off switch. One of the three small knobs is a dummy knob.
There is a dent on the top of the headstock and some signs of touch-up paint, but overall, there is little noticeable damage and the guitar is in good condition.
It has been fully serviced and cleaned at our repair center, and playability is excellent.

item.description:
$580.46 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A Classic Progressive Masterpiece! The RD600!
The RD600 Transparent Black Sunburst has arrived!
The RD600 debuted as a model that preserved the traditional sound of its era while delivering the punchy low end that has become the cornerstone of modern bass tones.
Now, more than 20 years later, basses capable of producing a wide variety of tones have emerged, and playing styles have evolved with the times...
However, the well-balanced low end?which remains within the audible range without being overly boomy?combined with its edgy punch, is a charm unique to instruments from that era, making this a true classic.
Although there is some tarnish on the metal parts and a few minor scratches, it is in clean condition considering its age.
Donft miss this opportunity to own it!

5-string bass in perfect condition, purchased in early 2026.

item.title: YAMAHA TRBX304 CAR Candy Apple Red [IJM283230] (06/29) — $406.32
item.description:
$406.32 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

This is a Fender Japan Jazz Bass JB62-58, manufactured between 1997 and 2000 during the gred tortoiseshell pickguardh era.

A 60s-style Jazz Bass that follows the classic f62 style. It features a basswood body, a slab-joined rosewood fingerboard, JB Single (JAPAN) pickups, and a 34-inch long scale with 20 frets.

$1,302.80 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

The AMAZE delivers an all-around sound despite its compact size.
The body is made of alder, the neck is hard maple, the fingerboard is rosewood, and it features an ESP Custom Lab J-1 pickup and an active 3-band EQ (CINNAMON).

We have corrected the nut grooves, fixed the seized set screw on the 2nd-string saddle and replaced the spring, and leveled the frets.

The Aria Pro II 4-String Jazz Bass gJB-600h is now in stock!
This model features a vintage style strongly reminiscent of the late 1970s, combined with the high-quality craftsmanship unique to Japanese-made instruments.
It features SEN WOOD, maple neck, and rosewood fingerboard.

This is the mid-range model of Yamahafs flagship BB bass series.
The miter-bolted neck joint creates a powerful, resonant sound thatfs fully integrated with the body.
It features a convertible-type bridge and a 5-piece neck construction, carrying on the BB tradition.
It features an alder body, a 5-piece neck made of maple and mahogany, and a rosewood fingerboard.
It is equipped with VSP5n and VSC5b pickups in a PJ configuration.
Controls consist of 2 volume and 1 tone knobs.

There is a small dent near the body end and some minor playwear on the pickguard, but overall, it shows very little signs of use.
The nut, frets, and truss rod are all in good condition.
It has been adjusted by our repair department since arrival. This is a guitar that still has plenty of life left in it!!
